sources.name: GDELT
sources.notes: Use for topic detection, clustering, geography hints, and media-attention bursts. Do not use as the sole basis for a final bet.
sources.license_notes: Free/open platform; verify reuse terms per downstream workflow.

sources.name: World Bank Indicators API
sources.notes: Use for structural baselines and country comparisons.
sources.license_notes: Open World Bank data; confirm per-series usage terms if redistributed.

sources.name: ILOSTAT Bulk Download Facility
sources.notes: Use the official bulk indicator CSV downloads for labour structure, participation, unemployment, sector dynamics, and informality.
sources.license_notes: Public bulk download access exposed by ILOSTAT; verify per-dataset reuse terms if redistributed.

sources.name: USGS Earthquake Feeds
sources.notes: Use for precise hazard confirmation with timestamps and magnitude.
sources.license_notes: Machine-readable feeds exposed publicly by USGS.

sources.name: OpenStreetMap Overpass API
sources.update_cadence: Near real time with some lag
sources.notes: Use for infrastructure density, place context, proximity checks, and asset lookup.
sources.license_notes: Read-only API over OSM data; respect OSM/instance usage expectations.

sources.name: EIA API
sources.coverage: Energy markets, electricity, fuels, and system data
sources.notes: Use for energy system context and supply-demand stress.
sources.license_notes: Free API, but requires an API key.

raw_signals.signal_title: Recent strong seismic activity near Indonesia
raw_signals.signal_summary: A recent high-magnitude quake keeps seismic exposure operationally live for Indonesian infrastructure and response systems. Strongest matching event magnitude: 5.3.

raw_signals.signal_title: Another recent significant quake near Indonesia
raw_signals.signal_summary: A second recent quake shows the signal is not just a single isolated event. Strongest matching event magnitude: 5.1.
A third recent quake reinforces that seismic volatility remains active in the region. Strongest matching event magnitude: 4.9.

future_bets.title: Seismic readiness systems in Indonesia
future_bets.summary: Repeated official earthquake activity suggests seismic readiness remains a live infrastructure priority in Indonesia.
future_bets.implication: Builders, utilities, logistics operators, and public-sector teams should treat inspection, monitoring, and response systems as active demand rather than a background concern.
future_bets.why_now: A cluster of recent official quakes matters because it keeps resilience spending tied to current operational risk, not abstract history.

paired_signals.why_it_matters: Repeated official quake activity sustains urgency around resilience, inspection, and emergency logistics.
paired_signals.connection: Multiple recent quakes in the same national footprint strengthen the case for practical readiness systems.

paired_signals.signal_title: Another recent significant quake near Indonesia
paired_signals.why_it_matters: Repeated official quake activity sustains urgency around resilience, inspection, and emergency logistics.
paired_signals.connection: Multiple recent quakes in the same national footprint strengthen the case for practical readiness systems.

second_order_effects: Inspection, monitoring, and emergency communications tools become easier to justify.
Procurement for retrofit and response capacity may stay active longer than a single-event spike.
what_to_watch: Further official quakes in populated or infrastructure-heavy corridors.
Public or private resilience spending linked to inspection and emergency response.
what_could_change_this: A sustained quiet period could reduce urgency in the short term.
If funding tightens, readiness upgrades may be delayed even if risk remains.
